text.title: Problem Validation Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Problem Validation application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every evidence item MUST be classified into the hierarchy: paid (5) > committed (4) > engaged (3) > stated (2) > anecdote (1). Anecdote-only stacks below the kill threshold are KILL.
rule.rationale: Stated intent does not equal behaviour; paid behaviour is the only commitment that cannot be retracted cheaply.

rule.statement: Every interview question used to gather evidence MUST pass the Mom Test rewrite: no leading framing, no hypothetical futures, no validation-seeking. Questions about past behaviour only.
rule.rationale: Leading and hypothetical questions produce flattering lies; past-behaviour questions produce signal.

rule.statement: A kill threshold MUST be declared BEFORE evidence collection (e.g., "<3 paid intent signals from 10 interviews = KILL"). Post-hoc threshold adjustment is rejected.
rule.rationale: A movable goalpost guarantees PROCEED; pre-declaration is the only defence against motivated reasoning.

rule.statement: The artefact MUST emit exactly one of: PROCEED | PIVOT | KILL. Soft outcomes ("maybe", "needs more data") are rejected; if data is insufficient, the decision is KILL until more data is collected.
rule.rationale: Three-way typed decisions force commitment; soft outcomes let the team keep building on hope.

rule.statement: The evidence log MUST be attached to the artefact: ≥1 row per interview, hierarchy-tier classification, verbatim quote pointer. Loose summaries are rejected.
rule.rationale: The log is the audit trail; without it the decision cannot be replayed or contested.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
